fact: July 11 is exactly 192 days into the year, which means there are exactly 173 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat season - the sun stays up for over 15 hours in many places, making it perfect for epic water balloon fights!
Cicadas are screaming their loudest around July 11 in North America - some species buzz so loud they can damage your hearing if you get too close!
July 11 falls right in the middle of firefly mating season when they're desperately trying to find love by blinking like crazy!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, July 11 is smack in the middle of winter - penguins are huddling together while kids in America are swimming!
Corn plants in the Midwest United States are growing so fast around July 11 that you can literally hear them creaking and popping as they stretch toward the sky!
The sun rises before 6 AM and sets after 8 PM in most of Europe around July 11, giving kids almost 15 hours of daylight to play outside!
July 11 falls right when many flowers are producing their stinkiest, most disgusting smells to attract flies and beetles instead of pretty butterflies!
Ocean water temperatures typically hit their warmest point around this date in many coastal areas - perfect for spotting jellyfish floating near the surface!
In northern Canada and Alaska, the sun barely sets at all around July 11 - it just dips toward the horizon and bounces back up like a yo-yo!
Tree sap is flowing at its stickiest, gooiest peak around July 11 - touch a pine tree and you might get permanently glued to it!
July 11 is when many desert animals like rattlesnakes and scorpions become completely nocturnal because the daytime ground is literally too hot to touch!
